title: SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) Q1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: SolarEdge Technologies Q1 2024 Earnings Call Key Points. Financial Performance: Total Q1 revenues: 204.4 million (solar: 190.1 million, non-solar: 14.1 million). GAAP net loss: 157.3 million (-2.75 per share). Non-GAAP net loss: 8.6 million (-1.90 per share). Cash position: 950 million (expected lowest point for 2024). Operational Metrics: Shipped 1.1 million power optimizers, 69,000 inverters, 128 MWh batteries. Total shipments: approximately 950 megawatts (68% commercial, 32% residential). Inventory: 1.55 billion (619 inventory days, up from 386). Market Dynamics: U.S. Residential: Inverter/optimizer sell-through down 19% quarter-over-quarter; battery sales up 26% quarter-over-quarter, driven by California NEM 3.0 and Puerto Rico demand. Europe: Residential sell-through down 19% quarter-over-quarter. Germany slower than expected due to declining electricity prices. Guidance (Q2 2024): Revenues 250-280 million; gross margins -4% to 0% (including IRA benefits). Strategic Initiatives: Acquired Wevo for EV charging optimization software. Launching SolarEdge ONE for C&I energy management platform. Developing next-generation 20kW three-phase inverter for European market. Planning 30-50% cost reduction potential in next-generation inverters versus current generation.
